info.title: STOA Control-Plane API
info.description:
## STOA Platform API

**The European Agent Gateway** — AI-Native API Gateway for modern enterprises.

### Key Features

- **MCP Server Subscriptions** — Subscribe to AI tools and manage API keys
- **Tools Catalog** — Browse and discover MCP tools with role-based visibility
- **Usage Tracking** — Monitor your API consumption in real-time
- **Multi-tenant Management** — Create and manage isolated API tenants
- **GitOps Integration** — Automatic sync with GitLab repositories
- **Event-Driven Architecture** — Kafka/Redpanda message bus for async operations
- **Pipeline Monitoring** — End-to-end tracing of deployments
- **RBAC** — Role-based access control via Keycloak

### Quick Start

1. **Get a token** from Keycloak (`POST /realms/stoa/protocol/openid-connect/token`)
2. **Browse MCP servers** (`GET /v1/mcp/servers`)
3. **Subscribe to a server** (`POST /v1/mcp/subscriptions`)
4. **Use your API key** with MCP Gateway (`https://mcp.gostoa.dev`)

### Authentication

All endpoints require a valid JWT token from Keycloak (realm: `stoa`).
Include the token in the `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header.

```
Authorization: Bearer eyJhbGciOiJSUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9...
```

### Environments

| Environment | Base URL |
|-------------|----------|
| Production | `https://api.gostoa.dev` |
| Via Gateway | `https://apis.gostoa.dev/gateway/Control-Plane-API/2.0` |

### Changelog

#### v2.1.0 (Current)
- Added MCP Server Subscriptions with admin approval workflow
- Added API key rotation with grace period support
- Added Dashboard home page stats (`/v1/dashboard`)
- Added Service Accounts for M2M authentication

#### v2.0.0
- Added Pipeline Tracing (`/v1/traces`) for end-to-end monitoring
- Added GitLab Webhook integration (`/webhooks/gitlab`)
- Added Kafka integration for event-driven deployments
- Added Kafka integration for event-driven gateway orchestration

#### v1.0.0
- Initial release with tenant, API, and application management

get.description:
List error snapshots for the user's tenant.

Returns empty list when storage is unavailable.
Get snapshot statistics summary.

schema.title: Response Get Snapshot Stats Summary V1 Snapshots Stats Summary Get

get.description: Get snapshot statistics (alias for /stats/summary).

schema.title: Response Get Snapshot Stats V1 Snapshots Stats Get

get.description:
Get available filter values for the UI.

Returns empty filters when storage is unavailable.
Get detailed error snapshot by ID.

patch.description: Update the resolution status of an error snapshot.

post.description: Generate cURL command to replay the captured request.

get.description:
List tenants from database.

- CPI Admin: See all tenants
- Others: See only their own tenant

schema.title: Response List Tenants V1 Tenants Get

post.description:
Create a new tenant (CPI Admin only).

This will:
1. Create tenant in database with provisioning_status=PENDING
2. Fire async provisioning saga (KC group + admin user + policy seed + Kafka events)
3. Return immediately — poll provisioning-status for progress

get.description: Get tenant by ID from database (cached, TTL 30s).

delete.description:
Delete tenant (CPI Admin only).

Triggers async deprovisioning saga: disables policies, deletes KC group,
emits namespace cleanup event, archives tenant.

get.description: Get tenant provisioning status.

List APIs for a tenant from GitLab (paginated).

Optionally filter by environment — only returns APIs deployed to that environment.

post.description:
Create a new API in GitLab and emit event.

This creates the API definition in the GitOps repository:
- tenants/{tenant_id}/apis/{api_name}/api.yaml
- tenants/{tenant_id}/apis/{api_name}/openapi.yaml (if provided)

get.description: Get API by ID from GitLab
List all applications for a tenant (paginated).

post.description: Create a new application (Keycloak client).

get.description: Get application by ID.

post.description: Regenerate application client secret.
Subscribe application to an API.

get.description: List deployment history for a tenant

post.description:
Deploy an API to an environment.

Creates a deployment record, emits a deploy-request Kafka event,
and triggers webhook notifications.

get.description: Get deployment details

post.description: Rollback a deployment to a previous version

get.description:
Get deployment logs with optional cursor-based pagination.

Query params:
    after_seq: Return logs with seq > this value (for polling).
    limit: Max entries to return (default 200).
Get status of all APIs deployed in an environment
List recent commits for tenant repository

schema.title: Response List Commits V1 Tenants  Tenant Id  Git Commits Get

get.description: Get file content from GitLab

post.description: Create or update a file in GitLab

Merge a merge request

get.description: List branches

post.description: Create a new branch

get.description:
Stream all events (for cpi-admin users only).

This endpoint streams events from all tenants.
Only users with cpi-admin role can access this.

Get historical events for a tenant.

Event store (DB-backed) deferred — Kafka-only audit log has no query API.
See deployment history for recent activity.

post.description:
Receive deployment result notification from gateway adapters.

This endpoint is called by gateway adapters after completing
deployment, rollback, or other gateway operations.

The result is published to Kafka for:
- Real-time UI updates via SSE
- Audit logging
- Metrics collection

Handle GitLab webhooks for GitOps with full pipeline tracing.

Captures the git author (who pushed) from the GitLab payload and stores
traces in PostgreSQL for persistent monitoring.

get.description: Health check for GitLab webhook endpoint with trace stats from PostgreSQL.
List workflow templates for a tenant.

post.description: Create a new workflow template.

get.description: Get a workflow template by ID.

post.description: Start a new workflow instance from a template.

get.description: List workflow instances for a tenant.
Get a workflow instance by ID.

post.description: Approve the current step of a workflow instance.
Reject the current step of a workflow instance.

get.description: Get the audit trail for a workflow instance.

List recent pipeline traces with optional filtering.

Returns summaries for efficient list display.

Get pipeline trace statistics.

Returns aggregated metrics about pipeline executions.

Get currently running traces (in_progress status).

Useful for real-time monitoring dashboards.

Get detailed information about a specific trace.

Includes all steps with timing and error details.

Get a timeline view of a trace suitable for visualization.

Returns steps in a format optimized for timeline rendering.

post.description: Create a demo trace for testing the monitoring UI.
Create multiple demo traces for a realistic monitoring view.

get.description:
List APIs registered in the Gateway (paginated).

Requires: cpi-admin or devops role

post.description:
Import an API from OpenAPI specification into the Gateway.

Requires: cpi-admin or devops role with create permission

Args:
    apiName: Name for the API
    apiVersion: Version of the API
    url: URL to fetch OpenAPI spec from
    apiDefinition: OpenAPI spec content as string (alternative to url)
    type: API type (openapi, swagger, raml, wsdl)

get.description:
Get API details from Gateway.

Requires: cpi-admin or devops role

delete.description:
Delete an API from the Gateway.

Requires: cpi-admin role

put.description:
Activate an API in the Gateway.

Requires: cpi-admin or devops role with promote permission

Deactivate an API in the Gateway.

Requires: cpi-admin or devops role with promote permission

get.description:
List applications registered in the Gateway (paginated).

Requires: cpi-admin or tenant-admin role

List all OAuth scopes configured in the Gateway.

Requires: cpi-admin role

schema.title: Response List Gateway Scopes V1 Gateway Scopes Get

post.description:
Configure OIDC authentication for an API in the Gateway.

This high-level operation:
1. Creates an OAuth strategy for the API
2. Creates an application for the tenant
3. Creates scope mappings with standardized naming

Naming pattern: {AuthServer}:{TenantId}:{ApiName}:{Version}:{Scope}

Requires: cpi-admin role

get.description:
Check Gateway health status via OIDC proxy.

This validates that:
1. The Gateway-Admin-API proxy is accessible
2. The user's JWT token is valid for Gateway operations

post.description:
Create a new subscription request.

This endpoint is called by the Developer Portal when a user subscribes
to an API. The subscription starts in PENDING status and must be
approved by an API admin.

Returns the API key (shown only once!).

get.description:
List current user's subscriptions.

Used by the Developer Portal to show user's subscribed APIs.
Get subscription details by ID

delete.description:
Cancel a subscription (subscriber action).

Subscribers can cancel their own subscriptions.

post.description:
Rotate the API key for a subscription with grace period.

The old key remains valid for the specified grace period (default 24 hours).
During the grace period, both old and new keys are accepted.
After the grace period expires, only the new key is valid.

Returns the new API key (shown only once!) and grace period information.
An email notification is sent to the subscriber with the new key.

get.description:
Get subscription details with key rotation information.

Includes grace period status if a key rotation is in progress.

patch.description:
Extend the TTL of an active subscription.

Subscription owners can extend their expiry by 7 or 14 days.
Limited to 2 extensions and 60 total extended days.
Tenant admins and cpi-admins can extend any subscription in their scope.

get.description:
List all subscriptions for a tenant.

Used by tenant admins to view and manage subscriptions.

List pending subscriptions awaiting approval.

Used by tenant admins to see subscription requests.

post.description:
Approve a pending subscription.

Tenant admins approve subscription requests.

Revoke an active subscription.

Tenant admins can revoke subscriptions for policy violations.

Temporarily suspend a subscription.

Can be reactivated later.
Reactivate a suspended subscription.

Validate an API key (used by the Gateway).

This is an internal endpoint for the API Gateway to validate
incoming API keys and get subscription details.

Supports grace period: during key rotation, both old and new keys are valid.

get.description: List all available webhook event types with descriptions and payload examples.

post.description:
Create a new webhook configuration for a tenant.

Requires tenant-admin or cpi-admin role.

get.description: List all webhooks for a tenant.
Get a specific webhook configuration.
Get delivery history for a webhook.

parameters.name: limit

schema.description: Max number of deliveries to return
schema.title: Limit

parameters.description: Max number of deliveries to return

post.description: Manually retry a failed webhook delivery.
Send a test event to a webhook to verify it's working.

post.summary: Upload and validate a certificate file
post.description: Upload a .pem or .crt file for validation.

get.description: Check if certificate validation is available.

schema.title: Response Health Check V1 Certificates Health Get

get.description:
Search tools catalog with full-text search.

Supports:
- Full-text search across name, description, and tags
- Fuzzy matching for typo tolerance
- Faceted filtering by category, tags, and visibility
- Relevance scoring with boosted fields
- Pagination and sorting

Get auto-complete suggestions for tool names.

Returns matching tool names as the user types,
with fuzzy matching for typo tolerance.

Get all available facets for filtering tools.

Returns counts for each category, tag, and visibility level.

Get search analytics (admin only).

Returns:
- Top search queries
- Popular tools
- Search volume over time

Retourne le résumé d'usage pour l'utilisateur connecté.

Inclut:
- Stats aujourd'hui / cette semaine / ce mois
- Top 5 des tools les plus utilisés
- Evolution des appels sur 7 jours

Data Sources (CAB-840):
- Prometheus for metrics
- Falls back to empty data if Prometheus unavailable

Retourne la liste paginée des derniers appels MCP de l'utilisateur.

Filtres disponibles:
- status: success, error, timeout
- tool_id: filtrer par tool
- from_date / to_date: période

Data Sources (CAB-840):
- Loki for call logs
- Falls back to empty list if Loki unavailable

Retourne la liste des subscriptions actives de l'utilisateur.

Data Sources (CAB-840):
- PostgreSQL for subscription data
- Prometheus for usage counts (with DB fallback)

schema.title: Response Get My Active Subscriptions V1 Usage Me Subscriptions Get

get.description:
Return token consumption for the authenticated tenant.

Queries Prometheus for stoa_mcp_gateway_tokens_by_tenant metrics.
Used by `stoactl token-usage`.

CAB-881: Tenant-scoped — only returns data for the caller's tenant.

schema.title: Response Get Token Usage V1 Usage Tokens Get

get.description:
Compare token consumption before and after transformation.

Uses the transformer_reduction_ratio metric to compute savings.
Used by `stoactl token-usage --compare`.

schema.title: Response Get Token Usage Compare V1 Usage Tokens Compare Get

get.description:
Retourne les statistiques agrégées pour la home page du Portal.

Inclut:
- Nombre de tools disponibles
- Nombre de subscriptions actives
- Nombre d'appels API cette semaine
- Tendances (% change)

Data Sources (CAB-840):
- PostgreSQL for tool/subscription counts
- Prometheus for API call metrics and trends

Data Sources (CAB-840):
- Loki for activity logs
- Falls back to empty list if Loki unavailable
List all service accounts owned by the current user.

schema.title: Response List Service Accounts V1 Service Accounts Get

post.description: Create a new OAuth2 service account for MCP access. The account inherits your roles.

delete.description: Delete a service account. Only the owner can delete their accounts.

post.description: Generate a new client secret. The old secret will be invalidated immediately.

get.description:
Liveness probe - process is alive.

K8s will restart the pod if this fails.
Should be a simple check that the process is running.

Readiness probe - ready to accept traffic.

K8s will remove pod from service if this fails.
Checks critical dependencies (Kafka, Keycloak).
Non-critical: GitLab, Gateway.

Startup probe - initial boot complete.

K8s uses this during pod startup to know when
to start running liveness/readiness probes.

post.description:
Create a new Universal API Contract.

Creates the contract and initializes all protocol bindings as disabled.

get.description: List contracts for the user's tenant (cached, TTL 60s).

Returns all 5 protocols (REST, MCP, GraphQL, gRPC, Kafka) with their status.
Used by the Protocol Switcher UI component.

post.description:
Enable a protocol binding for a contract.

This triggers the UAC engine to generate the binding (endpoint, tool, etc.).

delete.description:

The binding is soft-disabled (keeps history). The endpoint becomes inactive.

get.description: Get MCP server detail with discovered tools.

get.description:
List all promoted APIs available in the Portal catalog.

Source: PostgreSQL cache (synced from GitLab)

By default, only returns APIs with portal_published=true.
Set include_unpromoted=true to see all APIs (for admin/debugging).

Returns APIs from all tenants the user has access to.

Performance: Uses PostgreSQL cache - <200ms response time.

parameters.name: include_unpromoted

schema.description: Include APIs not promoted to Portal
schema.title: Include Unpromoted

parameters.description: Include APIs not promoted to Portal

schema.description: Filter by audience: public, internal, partner
schema.title: Audience

parameters.description: Filter by audience: public, internal, partner

Source: PostgreSQL cache (synced from GitLab)

Note: api_id format is "{tenant_id}/{api_name}" or just "{api_name}"

Get OpenAPI specification for an API.

Source: PostgreSQL cache (synced from GitLab)

Returns the cached OpenAPI spec (originally from openapi.yaml or openapi.json).

List all MCP servers available in the Portal catalog.

Source: PostgreSQL cache (synced from GitLab via GitOps — CAB-689)

Returns only active servers that the user can see based on visibility rules.
All filtering, sorting, and pagination is done in SQL.
Includes synced_at timestamp for cache freshness tracking.
Falls back to Git sync if cache is empty (Obligation #3).
Get list of available API categories from the catalog cache.

schema.title: Response Get Api Categories V1 Portal Api Categories Get

schema.title: Response Get Api Universes V1 Portal Api Universes Get

Internal endpoint — no JWT auth required (service-to-service on internal network).
Used by stoa-gateway to discover APIs and register them as MCP tools.

List user's applications.

Returns all applications owned by the current user across all tenants
they have access to.

post.description:
Create a new application.

Returns the application with client_secret (only shown once!).
Creates a corresponding Keycloak OAuth client if KC is available.

get.description: Get application by ID.

post.description:
Regenerate application client secret.

Returns the new client_secret (only shown once!).
Requires Keycloak client to be linked.

Trigger a full sync of all MCP servers from GitLab.

This will:
1. Read all MCP server definitions from GitLab
2. Create/update servers in the database
3. Mark servers not in GitLab as orphans

Requires: cpi-admin role

Trigger sync of all MCP servers for a specific tenant.

Use tenant_id="_platform" for platform-level servers.

Requires: cpi-admin role

Trigger sync of a specific MCP server.

Requires: cpi-admin role

get.description:
Get current GitOps sync status.

Shows:
- Total servers in database
- Count by sync status (synced, pending, error, orphan)

Requires: cpi-admin role

Check GitLab connection health.

Requires: cpi-admin role

List all MCP servers defined in GitLab (before sync to DB).

Useful for debugging and verifying GitLab content.

Requires: cpi-admin role

List all available MCP tools.

Proxies to MCP Gateway with user context for role-based filtering.

Get all available tool tags with counts.

Proxies to MCP Gateway.

Get all available tool categories with counts.

Proxies to MCP Gateway.

Get details of a specific tool.

Proxies to MCP Gateway.

Get the input schema for a tool.

Proxies to MCP Gateway.

post.description:
Invoke a tool with the provided arguments.

Proxies to MCP Gateway with user context.

Dry-run policy check for tool invocation.

Validates arguments against all matching policies without executing the tool.
Proxies to MCP Gateway.

Use cases:
- Pre-flight validation in AI agents
- Policy testing and debugging
- Demo scenarios showing STOA governance

get.description:
List all active policy rules.

Returns metadata about loaded policies including name, tool pattern,
priority, and enabled status. Proxies to MCP Gateway.

Use cases:
- Understanding what policies are in effect
- Debugging policy configuration
- Demo scenarios showing governance rules

List recent API transactions.

Returns transaction summaries for efficient list display.

Get API transaction statistics.

Returns aggregated metrics about API calls.

Get detailed information about a specific transaction.

Includes all spans with timing and metadata.

get.description: Get onboarding funnel analytics with conversion rates and TTFTC stats.
Get users who started onboarding but haven't completed within the threshold.

schema.title: Response Get Stalled Users V1 Admin Onboarding Stalled Get

Get current platform status including GitOps sync state and recent events.

schema.title: Response List Platform Events V1 Platform Events Get

Args:

post.description:
Trigger a full catalog synchronization from GitLab.

This operation runs in the background and syncs all APIs from all tenants.
Use GET /sync/status to check progress.

Trigger MCP servers synchronization from GitLab.

CAB-689: Syncs server.yaml files from Git to the mcp_servers table.

Trigger catalog synchronization for a specific tenant.

Get the status of the last catalog sync operation.

Returns information about the most recent sync including:

delete.description:
Invalidate the in-memory API cache (if any).

Note: This endpoint is maintained for backwards compatibility.
With the new PostgreSQL-based cache, the sync endpoints should be used instead.

post.description:
Seed APIs directly into the catalog cache (bypasses GitLab).

Use this when GitLab is not connected (e.g., demo environments,
local development). Inserts or updates entries in the api_catalog table.

List prospects with filters and pagination.

Returns a paginated list of prospects with their current status,
NPS score (if submitted), and event counts.

**Requires**: `cpi-admin` role

Get aggregated KPIs for the prospects dashboard.

Returns:
- Conversion funnel (invited → opened → converted)

**Requires**: `cpi-admin` role

get.description:
Export prospects to CSV file.

Downloads a CSV file with all prospects matching the filters.
The file includes: email, company, status, source, dates,
time-to-tool metrics, and NPS feedback.

**Requires**: `cpi-admin` role

Get detailed prospect info with timeline.

Returns full prospect details including:
- Basic info (email, company, status)
- NPS feedback (if submitted)
- Calculated metrics (time-to-tool, session duration)
- Event timeline (last 50 events)
- Error events (separated for easy viewing)

**Requires**: `cpi-admin` role

Get real-time operations metrics from Prometheus.

Requires admin, cpi-admin, or devops role.

Returns platform-wide metrics:
- error_rate: 5-minute error rate percentage
- p95_latency_ms: 95th percentile latency
- requests_per_minute: Current request rate
- active_alerts: Number of firing alerts
- uptime: Platform availability percentage

Get business analytics metrics.

Requires cpi-admin or admin role.

Returns:
- active_tenants: Number of active tenants in the platform
- new_tenants_30d: Tenants created in the last 30 days
- tenant_growth: Growth rate percentage
- apdex_score: Platform APDEX score (from Prometheus)
- total_tokens: Total tokens consumed (from Prometheus)
- total_calls: Total API calls (from Prometheus)

Get top APIs/tools by usage.

Requires cpi-admin or admin role.

Returns top N tools by invocation count over the last 30 days.

schema.title: Response Get Top Apis V1 Business Top Apis Get

get.description:
Get current user information and calculated permissions.

This endpoint is the **single source of truth** for user permissions.
Frontend applications (Portal, Console) should call this endpoint
after authentication to get the user's permissions and scopes.

**Response includes:**
- `user_id`: Keycloak subject ID
- `email`: User email
- `username`: Preferred username
- `tenant_id`: Associated tenant (may be null for platform admins)
- `roles`: Keycloak realm roles (filtered, no system roles)
- `permissions`: Granular permissions derived from roles (e.g., apis:read)
- `effective_scopes`: OAuth2 scopes derived from roles (e.g., stoa:catalog:read)

**Usage in Frontend:**
```typescript
const { data } = await api.get('/v1/me');
// Check permission: data.permissions.includes('apis:create')
// Check scope: data.effective_scopes.includes('stoa:catalog:write')
```

post.description:

No permission required — any authenticated user without a tenant can call this.

post.description:
Gateway self-registration endpoint.

Called by STOA gateways at startup. Creates or updates the gateway instance
record based on the deterministic name derived from hostname + mode + environment.

Returns the assigned gateway ID for subsequent heartbeat calls.

Gateway heartbeat endpoint.

Called every 30s by STOA gateways to maintain ONLINE status.
If no heartbeat received for 90s, gateway is marked OFFLINE by the health worker.

get.description:
Get configuration for a registered gateway.

Future: Return pending deployments, policies, etc. for the gateway to apply.
Currently returns basic gateway info.

List available environments with their configuration.

Returns environment configs including mode (full, read-only, promote-only)
which determines what actions are allowed in each environment.

post.description: Create a new consumer for a tenant.

get.description: List consumers for a tenant with optional filtering.
Get consumer details by ID.

post.description: Suspend an active consumer.
Reactivate a suspended consumer.
Block a consumer (permanent until unblocked).

get.description:
Get consumer OAuth2 credentials (one-time secret display).

Regenerates the client secret each time it is called so the secret
is only visible once per request.  The caller must store it securely.

post.description:
Exchange a token via RFC 8693 using a consumer's Keycloak client.

The consumer's OAuth2 client acts as the acting party in the exchange.
The exchanged token inherits the consumer's protocol-mapped claims
(tenant_id, consumer_id, plan_slug).

Bulk onboard consumers from a CSV file (CAB-864).

CSV columns: external_id, name, email, company (optional), certificate_pem (optional).
Max 100 rows per request. Each row is processed independently.

Bind an initial mTLS certificate to a consumer (CAB-872).

Use this when a consumer has no certificate yet, or when the previous
certificate was revoked/expired and needs replacement.
Returns 409 if consumer already has an active or rotating certificate.

Revoke a consumer's mTLS certificate (CAB-864).

Sets certificate_status to 'revoked' and disables the Keycloak client.
Idempotent — revoking an already-revoked certificate is a no-op.

Rotate a consumer's mTLS certificate (CAB-864).

Accepts a new PEM certificate, moves the current fingerprint to
certificate_fingerprint_previous, and sets a grace period.

get.description:
List consumers with certificates expiring within N days (CAB-872).

Also auto-expires any active certificates that are past their not_after date.

post.description:
Revoke certificates for multiple consumers at once (CAB-872).

Idempotent — already-revoked or no-cert consumers are skipped.

get.description: Get a consumer's current quota status (usage + remaining + resets).
